Is there a situation where that was happening and you were able to try to find ways to avoid those types of meetings from happening?
Yeah.
At this business, this architectural business, we would have a team meeting every Monday in which everyone would go by everything they did.
And it made no sense.
I'm talking like the architects were hearing marketing people speak and they couldn't hear less.
So what I suggested is that we divided the meetings into three groups, architects, marketing and sales.
So we have three meetings.
I have to join all of them because my job is related to all of them.
But now the team gets to focus on their job and marketing girls talk.
About marketing, salespeople, focus on sales, and sometimes we have to combine because sometimes for something related to marketing, we need the architect, but it's a shorter meeting.
So that optimizes a lot, and I feel like people are in a better spirit to actually join the meeting, tell the things, the work, and it works, I guess.
Yeah.
